Sexually Transmitted Infections Treatment Guidelines, 2021

Adolescents

Related Pages 
In the United States, prevalence rates of certain STIs are highest among adolescents and young adults ( 141 ). For example, reported rates of chlamydia and gonorrhea are highest among females during their adolescent and young adult years, and many persons acquire HPV infection during that time.
Persons who initiate sex early in adolescence are at higher risk for STIs, as are adolescents living in detention facilities; those receiving services at STD clinics; those who are involved in commercial sex exploitation or survival sex and are exchanging sex for drugs, money, food, or housing; young males who have sex with m
